_comment: Curated, date-accurate GFC headlines (paraphrased from the historical record). 'tone' drives the dramatic irony: reassuring/euphoric headlines fire while the chart cracks. Shown at the bar whose date is >= the headline date.

headlines.text: BNP Paribas freezes three funds, citing an inability to value subprime assets. Credit markets seize up overnight.
Stocks close at an all-time record high. Wall Street strategists see more room to run.
Bank of America to acquire Countrywide. Officials say the mortgage trouble looks contained.
Bear Stearns receives emergency financing as it runs out of cash. Firm insists its balance sheet is sound.
JPMorgan to buy Bear Stearns for $2 a share — a 90%+ wipeout from Friday. The Fed backstops the deal.
Fed officials suggest the worst of the credit crisis may now be behind us. Markets rebound.
IndyMac seized by regulators — one of the largest bank failures in U.S. history.
U.S. government takes over Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac. Stocks rally on hopes the worst is now ring-fenced.
Lehman Brothers files for bankruptcy — the largest in U.S. history. No buyer, no bailout.
Fed seizes AIG with an $85 billion loan to prevent a global cascade.
Treasury unveils a $700 billion rescue plan. Markets soar; some declare the bottom is in.
House REJECTS the bailout. The Dow plunges 777 points — its largest one-day point drop ever.
Congress passes the $700B TARP rescue. Relief rally fades within hours.
Worst week in Wall Street history. Selling is relentless; nothing seems to stop it.
Historic rally: Dow surges 936 points as governments inject capital into banks.
Stocks crater to an 11-year low. Fear of a second Great Depression grips markets.
Markets sink on Inauguration Day as fresh doubts swirl around the banking system.
A stimulus bill is signed — and stocks keep falling. Capitulation everywhere.
The index touches 666 intraday — its lowest level since 1996. Bulls have given up.
A leaked Citigroup memo says it was profitable in Jan–Feb. The market explodes higher (+379 Dow).
Treasury's plan to buy toxic assets ignites a massive rally. The tide may finally be turning.
